Module: A - What is CUI and why it matters
Learning Outcome: Define CUI and give examples relevant to CivicActions (vulnerability scan results, contractor bids/proposals, non-public contract data); explain the need-to-know principle for CUI access

Take steps to prevent unauthorized access to or observation of CUI; protect CUI confidentiality on federal and limited-access CivicActions systems; contact CISO/CTO or #loving-security if requirements are unclear

Do not transfer, print, or save CUI outside the CivicActions CUI Security Boundary; delete copies as soon as no longer needed; include CUI audit in project closeout procedures; report suspected compromise as a security incident

Identify the three approved CUI locations: secured client network, CivicActions Google Workspace (access-controlled Shared Drives marked "CUI"), and CivicActions Workstations (managed device or approved hardened BYOD); understand that Google Workspace auto-tags documents containing "CUI"

Apply correct CUI markings when sharing outside a security boundary: "CONTROLLED UNCLASSIFIED INFORMATION" or "CUI" in the header, "Sensitive in accordance with 32 CFR 2002" centered at bottom of first page, and optional originator line per agency
